channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Self-help author James Arthur Ray, whose seminar led to tragedy and death, dies at 67
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — James Arthur Ray, a self-help guru whose multimillion-dollar business toppled after he led a sweat lodge ceremony in Arizona that left three people dead, has died. He was 67.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, KREX 5

item.title: Shooting near Honduran Consulate in Atlanta leaves 1 dead, 1 injured; suspect arrested
item.description: The Doraville Police Department in Georgia said one person was killed and another wounded on Monday after gunshots rang out in the Honduran Consulate parking lot near Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A 3-year-old boy's death in Honolulu fireworks explosion raises toll to 4
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A 3-year-old boy who died Monday was the fourth person to succumb to injuries from a massive explosion of fireworks on New Year's Eve at a Honolulu home, and a doctor said six others faced long recoveries in Arizona where they were sent to be treated for severe burns.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hurdles remain as Israel and Hamas once again inch toward a ceasefire deal
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Hamas once again appear to be inching toward a ceasefire that could wind down the 15-month war in Gaza and bring home dozens of Israelis held hostage there. Both Israel and Hamas are under pressure from outgoing U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial this spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: After 2 years of shortfalls, California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit
item.description: TURLOCK, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris calls it a 'good day' for democracy as she oversees peaceful certification of Trump's victory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was no drama on Monday as Kamala Harris oversaw the ceremonial certification of her defeat to Donald Trump. The vice president kept her remarks perfunctory, and she stood with her hands clasped in front of her while the results from each state were read out loud.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: British prime minister condemns ‘lies and misinformation’ after Musk attacks
item.description: Elon Musk has criticized Keir Starmer, called for new elections in the U.K. and voiced support for far-right figures in the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's is the latest company to roll back diversity goals
item.description: McDonald’s is ending some of its diversity practices, citing a U.S. Supreme Court decision that outlawed affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: A federal judge in New York City has found Rudy Giuliani in contempt of court for failing to properly respond to information requests in a $148 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Virginia Beach teacher attacked by student at Independence Middle School
item.description: According to Virginia Beach City Public Schools, a student at Independence Middle School physically attacked a teacher inside a classroom.
item.dc:creator: Chrysnel Banzouzi, NewsNation

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden issues ban on offshore oil and gas drilling in most federal waters. Trump vows to undo it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is moving to ban new offshore oil and gas drilling in most U.S. coastal waters, a last-minute effort to block possible action by the incoming Trump administration to expand offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione hires Harvey Weinstein’s prison consultant Craig Rothfeld: report
item.description: UnitedHealthcare murder suspect Luigi Mangione has retained Craig Rothfeld, a former white-collar inmate turned prison consultant who also worked for Harvey Weinstein.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High school teacher arrested for selling THC, nicotine vape cartridges to students: police
item.description: A former Virginia high school teacher is charged with selling vape cartridges containing THC and nicotine to multiple students at Falls Church High School.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star choked, punched woman before Myrtle Beach arrest, report says
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: College students charged with ambushing US soldier in 'To Catch a Predator' TikTok scheme: police
item.description: An Assumption University student who met the 22-year-old Army soldier was 18 and indicated this on her Tinder profile. Regardless, police said about 30 students ambushed the man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope selects liberal California cardinal as new DC Catholic leader ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Pope Francis selected San Diego Cardinal Robert McElroy, who once called for anti-Trump "disruptors," to lead the Catholic Church in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winter weather can disrupt. Here's what to do if your flight is canceled
item.description: Winter weather often causes flight delays and cancellations. While airlines can’t control the weather, they are required in the U.S. to provide refunds to customers whose flights are canceled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman warning others after accidentally gluing eye shut with nail glue
item.description: A woman is warning others after she accidentally glued her eye shut when she mistook her eye drops for nail glue.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bowyer, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: Congress certifies Trump's election victory
item.description: President-elect Trump's election victory will be certified on Monday, marking a sense of vindication for his team and a blow to his critics, while Kamala Harris is preparing to affirm his victory and Elon Musk extends his political influence over Europe.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Florence woman kidnapped 1-year-old, abandoned it overnight in near-freezing temperatures, police say
item.description: FLORENCE, S.C. (WBTW) -- A Florence woman allegedly kidnapped a 1-year-old Thursday evening and then abandoned the baby about two hours later in near-freezing temperatures outside a home on Cale Yarborough Highway in the Timmonsville area, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, NewsNation

item.title: Judge denies Trump's bid to halt Friday's hush money case sentencing while they appeal to block it
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump was thwarted Monday in his bid to indefinitely postpone this week’s sentencing in his hush money case while he appeals a ruling that upheld the verdict.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris readies to certify Trump’s election win – and her decisive defeat
item.description: Kamala Harris is readying on Monday to certify the 2024 election results, affirming President-elect Trump’s victory as well as her decisive defeat to him after a whirlwind run for commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden visits makeshift memorial in New Orleans where attack began that killed 14 and injured 30
item.description: President Joe Biden has visited a makeshift memorial at the site of the deadly New Year’s attack in New Orleans as he visits the city to meet with grieving families.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man behind New Year's attack visited New Orleans before, recorded video with smart glasses, FBI says
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The man responsible for the truck attack in New Orleans on New Year's Day that killed 14 people visited the city twice before and recorded video of the French Quarter with Meta smart glasses, an FBI official said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, STEPHEN SMITH and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mariota leads playoff-bound Commanders to 23-19 win over Cowboys after replacing Daniels
item.description: Marcus Mariota threw a 5-yard touchdown pass to Terry McLaurin with 3 seconds left, lifting Washington to a 23-19 victory over the Dallas Cowboys on Sunday and clinching the sixth seed in the NFC playoffs for the Commanders.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 dead and 19 injured in Southern California plane crash
item.description: FULLERTON, Calif. (AP) — Two people died and 19 were injured Thursday when a small plane crashed through the roof of a sprawling furniture manufacturing building in Southern California where at least 200 people were working, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, WGN 9
